chapter_title.ar: باب الْخُشُوعِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ وَ كَرَاهِيَةِ الْعَبَثِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – The humbleness during the Salāt and abhorrence of the frivolities

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/79)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/79)
text: إِذَا قُمْتَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَلَا تَعْبَثْ بِلِحْيَتِكَ وَ لَا بِرَأْسِكَ وَ لَا تَعْبَثْ بِالْحَصَى وَ أَنْتَ تُصَلِّي إِلَّا أَنْ تُسَوِّيَ حَيْثُ تَسْجُدُ فَإِنَّهُ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, raising it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Whenever you stand regarding the Salāt, so neither play around with your beard, nor with your head, nor with the pebbles while you are praying Salāt, unless you prepare (pebbles) as you are about to do Sajdah, so it is not a problem’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa in a marfu‘ manner who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that when you stand up for Salah (prayer), you must not play with your beard, your head or with pebbles while you pray, except when you prepare (pebbles) as you are about to do sajdah in which case it is not an offense.”
